body:
'[late embryo](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000323)' does not carry any sign that it should be reserved to some taxa (no explicit taxon restriction, and nothing in the definition that suggests it is not applicable to all metazoans).

However, it is defined through '[late embryonic stage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0007220)', which is itself '[preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062)' some '[pharyngula stage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004707)'.

The term “pharyngula” seems specific to vertebrates (or, at the very least, chordates). See for example:

* [Masak & Davidson (2023)](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cdev.2023.203866): “Pharyngula: the stage in early vertebrate development when embryos share phenotypic characteristics”
* [Collazo (2000)](https://doi.org/10.1080/10635150050207357): “the pharyngula (the phylotypic and zootypic stage of craniates)”
* Ballard (1964): “pharyngula: a postneural stage characterized by the presence of pharyngeal clefts or grooves.” (Pharyngeal arches are themselves specific to chordates, so if they characterise the pharyngula stage, then it follows that the pharyngula stage is chordate-specific).

Furthermore, the '[pharyngula stage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004707)' is '[preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062)' some '[neurula stage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000110)'.

Though '[neurula stage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000110)' in Uberon is again not explicitly taxon-restricted, as far as I know, “neurula“ and “neurulation” are only ever used to refer to chordates. In GO, “neurulation” is an exact synonym for '[neural tube formation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0001841)'; the concept of “neural tube“ is specific to chordates. In fact, Uberon’s '[neurula stage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000110)' is '[simultaneous with](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002082)' some '[neural tube formation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0001841)'. Through that relationship, the stage is ultimately linked to the '[notochord](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002328)', which is obviously and almost by definition specific to chordates.

The bottom line is that '[late embryo](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000323)' and '[late embryonic stage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0007220)', which _should_ be applicable broadly to all metazoans, are ultimately linked to chordate-specific terms.

The only reason this does not currently cause any violations of the taxon constraints is because the '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' relation does _not_ hold over most of the “temporal” relations (it only holds over '[existence ends during](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002492)' and '[existence starts during](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002488)'). I think that relation should actually hold over most if not all of the temporal relations (and I plan to propose that to the RO folks),and if/when it does, then the taxon constraint violations in Uberon will become apparent.

**I therefore propose to:**

1. Explicitly mark '[neurula stage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000110)' and '[pharyngula stage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004707)' as being taxon-restricted to chordates ('[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some [Chordata](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_7711));
2. Change the relationship between '[late embryonic stage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0007220)' and '[pharyngula stage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004707)' from

> '[late embryonic stage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0007220)' **SubClassOf** '[preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062)' **some** '[pharyngula stage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004707)'

to

> ('[late embryonic stage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0007220)' **and** '[occurs in](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000066)' **some** [Chordata](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_7711)) **SubClassOf** '[preceded by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000062)' **some** '[pharyngula stage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004707)'

(i.e. make that relationship a GCI; roughly the same approach as used in #2829).
title: 'late embryo' connected to effectively vertebrate-specific stages
